sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.11 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group and suppose that there exists a non-empty proper subset$$of the set of all primes dividing$|G|$such that the centralizer of every non-trivial$$-ele\-ment is a$$-sub\-group. Does it follow that$G$contains a subgroup$U$such that$U^g U=1$or$U$for every$g G$, and the centralizer of every non-trivial element of~$U$is contained in$U$? { K.\,W.\,Gruenberg} Yes, it does (mod CFSG) ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
